permissions.__defaultMode_options: Options: (unset)=ask-every-time, 'dontAsk'=allow-list-only, 'auto'=AI-classifier, 'bypassPermissions'=no-checks. BEGINNERS: leave unset (delete the defaultMode line) so Claude asks permission for every action until you trust your setup.
permissions.__defaultMode_WARNING: dontAsk silently denies unlisted tools with no error message. New users should remove this line and learn the permission system before enabling it.
permissions.__additionalDirectories_note: Replace paths below with YOUR home directory. Windows: C:\Users\YourUser\.claude | macOS: /Users/youruser/.claude | Linux: /home/youruser/.claude. Tilde (~) is NOT supported here -- use full absolute paths.

environment: Cloud provider(s): YOUR_CLOUD (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure)

hooks.prompt: An MCP tool call failed. Common causes: 1) MCP server crashed -- try once more. 2) Docker not running (docker MCP). 3) Browser closed (playwright MCP). If retry fails, fall back to non-MCP alternatives (curl for web, docker CLI for containers).

Compaction is imminent. Ensure your current todo items, active file paths, pending verifications, and current plan step are included in the compacted summary. State snapshot saved at ~/.claude/precompact-state.json -- read it to restore context about the active plan and working state.

Context was just compacted. CRITICAL: 1) Check your todo list and resume in-progress tasks. 2) Re-read the active plan file if one exists. 3) Key rules from CLAUDE.md still apply -- re-read it if needed. 4) If you lost track, read the session checkpoint at ~/.claude/session-checkpoint.txt. 5) State snapshot at ~/.claude/precompact-state.json -- read it to restore context about the active plan and working state.

Stop.__cost_note: This Stop hook runs Sonnet on EVERY response. In a 50-response session, this adds ~50 Sonnet calls (cost impact: see TROUBLESHOOTING.md#cost-higher-than-expected). Remove if cost is a concern.

hooks.prompt: If the last response contained no code, no file edits, no bash commands, and no git operations -- just conversation or explanation -- respond {"ok": true} immediately without further analysis. Otherwise, review the last response for these issues: 1) Security vulnerabilities: SQL injection, XSS, command injection, exposed secrets or hardcoded credentials. 2) ORM models referenced that don't exist in the schema. 3) Project-specific violations from CLAUDE.md (check soft-delete patterns, API conventions, navigation patterns). 4) Unauthorized git push commands (check CLAUDE.md for push policy). 5) Verification gap: if code was written or a feature was implemented but NO verification step was performed (no test run, no endpoint check, no file re-read to confirm changes), this is a violation. Respond with JSON only: {"ok": true} or {"ok": false, "reason": "concise description of the issue"}
